content.title: Flowboard
content.short_description: Flowboard is a modern, feature-rich project management application inspired by Trello, built with React, Next.js, and TypeScript. It provides teams with a flexible workspace to organize tasks through multiple view modes including Kanban boards, Timeline (Gantt-style), Calendar, and Table views.
content.description: Flowboard is a comprehensive project management solution that offers teams multiple ways to visualize and organize their work. The application features four switchable views (Kanban, Timeline, Calendar, Table), advanced drag-and-drop functionality, real-time collaboration capabilities, and robust data management. Built with modern web technologies, it provides a responsive, accessible interface that works seamlessly across devices while maintaining data persistence and offering extensive customization options.
content.role: As the lead developer and architect, I designed and built the entire Flowboard platform from scratch. I implemented the complete React/Next.js application architecture, created all four view modes (Kanban, Timeline, Calendar, Table), developed the sophisticated drag-and-drop system using @dnd-kit, and built the board sharing and collaboration features. I handled the full-stack development including state management with Zustand, UI component design with Radix primitives, form validation with React Hook Form and Zod, and responsive styling with Tailwind CSS. I also managed the deployment process to Netlify and ensured the application met production-ready standards for performance and accessibility.

achievements: Successfully deployed production-ready project management platform
Implemented efficient drag-and-drop system with optimistic updates
Created four distinct view modes for different project workflows
Built board sharing system with invitation-based collaboration
Achieved responsive design optimized for desktop and mobile
Established robust data persistence with localStorage
technologies: React Hook Form with Zod validation

screenshots: Main application interface with all four view modes
Calendar view with task overflow modal
Card modal with rich editing capabilities
